data.title: Rocket Lab Expands Satellite Systems With Gauss Thruster And Mynaric Deal
data.description:
• Rocket Lab introduced its new Gauss electric satellite thruster, designed for high volume constellation deployments.
  • The company completed its acquisition of Mynaric, adding laser optical communications technology and expanding its customer reach.
  • These moves further extend Rocket Lab’s role beyond launch services into a broader supplier of satellite subsystems.

Rocket Lab (NasdaqCM:RKLB) is leaning further into space systems at a time when interest in satellite constellations remains high. The company’s share price stands at $73.6, with a very large 1 year return and a multi year performance that is several times higher than three years ago. This highlights how closely investors have been watching execution. In that context, Gauss and the Mynaric deal provide new developments to track within the company’s core capabilities.

For investors, a key question is how effectively Rocket Lab can scale production of Gauss and integrate Mynaric’s optical communications into its broader offering. These steps indicate a business model that is increasingly focused on recurring satellite hardware and services in addition to launch. Future updates on contract activity, production capacity and customer adoption will be important to monitor.

data.title: Analysts Have Been Trimming Their Kasumigaseki Capital Co.,Ltd. (TSE:3498) Price Target After Its Latest Report
data.description:
Last week saw the newest half-yearly earnings release from Kasumigaseki Capital Co.,Ltd. (TSE:3498), an important milestone in the company's journey to build a stronger business. It was a workmanlike result, with revenues of JP¥61b coming in 3.8% ahead of expectations, and statutory earnings per share of JP¥520, in line with analyst appraisals. This is an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance in its report, look at what experts are forecasting for next year, and see if there has been any change to expectations for the business. Readers will be glad to know we've aggregated the latest statutory forecasts to see whether the analysts have changed their mind on Kasumigaseki CapitalLtd after the latest results.

Following the latest results, Kasumigaseki CapitalLtd's three analysts are now forecasting revenues of JP¥156.5b in 2026. This would be a substantial 26% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Statutory earnings per share are predicted to bounce 32% to JP¥687. Before this earnings report, the analysts had been forecasting revenues of JP¥166.9b and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥692 in 2026. So it looks like the analysts have become a bit less optimistic after the latest results announcement, with revenues expected to fall even as the company is supposed to maintain EPS.

View our latest analysis for Kasumigaseki CapitalLtd

It will come as no surprise then, that the consensus price target fell 5.2% to JP¥12,133following these changes. Fixating on a single price target can be unwise though, since the consensus target is effectively the average of analyst price targets. As a result, some investors like to look at the range of estimates to see if there are any diverging opinions on the company's valuation. The most optimistic Kasumigaseki CapitalLtd analyst has a price target of JP¥13,400 per share, while the most pessimistic values it at JP¥10,000. Still, with such a tight range of estimates, it suggeststhe analysts have a pretty good idea of what they think the company is worth.

Of course, another way to look at these forecasts is to place them into context against the industry itself. The analysts are definitely expecting Kasumigaseki CapitalLtd's growth to accelerate, with the forecast 60% annualised growth to the end of 2026 ranking favourably alongside historical growth of 45% per annum over the past five years. By contrast, our data suggests that other companies (with analyst coverage) in a similar industry are forecast to grow their revenue at 4.4% per year. It seems obvious that, while the growth outlook is brighter than the recent past, the analysts also expect Kasumigaseki CapitalLtd to grow faster than the wider industry.

The Bottom Line

The most obvious conclusion is that there's been no major change in the business' prospects in recent times, with the analysts holding their earnings forecasts steady, in line with previous estimates. Regrettably, they also downgraded their revenue estimates, but the latest forecasts still imply the business will grow faster than the wider industry. Even so, long term profitability is more important for the value creation process. data.title: Do Ebara's (TSE:6361) Earnings Warrant Your Attention?
data.description:
It's common for many investors, especially those who are inexperienced, to buy shares in companies with a good story even if these companies are loss-making. Sometimes these stories can cloud the minds of investors, leading them to invest with their emotions rather than on the merit of good company fundamentals. Loss-making companies are always racing against time to reach financial sustainability, so investors in these companies may be taking on more risk than they should.

In contrast to all that, many investors prefer to focus on companies like Ebara (TSE:6361), which has not only revenues, but also profits. Even if this company is fairly valued by the market, investors would agree that generating consistent profits will continue to provide Ebara with the means to add long-term value to shareholders.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Ebara's Earnings Per Share Are Growing

The market is a voting machine in the short term, but a weighing machine in the long term, so you'd expect share price to follow earnings per share (EPS) outcomes eventually. That makes EPS growth an attractive quality for any company. Over the last three years, Ebara has grown EPS by 15% per year. That growth rate is fairly good, assuming the company can keep it up.

One way to double-check a company's growth is to look at how its revenue, and earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) margins are changing. While we note Ebara achieved similar EBIT margins to last year, revenue grew by a solid 11% to JP¥958b. That's progress.

The chart below shows how the company's bottom and top lines have progressed over time. Click on the chart to see the exact numbers.

View our latest analysis for Ebara

The trick, as an investor, is to find companies that are going to perform well in the future, not just in the past. While crystal balls don't exist, you can check our visualization of consensus analyst forecasts for Ebara's future EPS 100% free.

Are Ebara Insiders Aligned With All Shareholders?

Since Ebara has a market capitalisation of JP¥2.4t, we wouldn't expect insiders to hold a large percentage of shares. But thanks to their investment in the company, it's pleasing to see that there are still incentives to align their actions with the shareholders. Indeed, they hold JP¥4.7b worth of its stock. That shows significant buy-in, and may indicate conviction in the business strategy. Even though that's only about 0.2% of the company, it's enough money to indicate alignment between the leaders of the business and ordinary shareholders.

Does Ebara Deserve A Spot On Your Watchlist?

As previously touched on, Ebara is a growing business, which is encouraging. If that's not enough on its own, there is also the rather notable levels of insider ownership. The combination definitely favoured by investors so consider keeping the company on a watchlist. We don't want to rain on the parade too much, but we did also find 2 warning signs for Ebara (1 is concerning!) that you need to be mindful of.

data.description:

Approach 1: SolarEdge Technologies Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes projected future cash flows and discounts them back to today to estimate what the entire business could be worth in present value terms.

For SolarEdge Technologies, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow sits at about $28.75 million. Analysts provide explicit forecasts for the next few years, and Simply Wall St then extends those estimates further. Within these projections, free cash flow is expected to reach $195 million in 2030, with intermediate years such as 2026, 2027 and 2028 also modeled in the $100 million to $200 million range, all expressed in dollars.

When these projected cash flows are discounted back to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $21.54 per share. Compared with the current share price of $37.83, this framework suggests the stock is 75.6% above the DCF-based estimate of intrinsic value.

Result: OVERVALUED

Approach 2: SolarEdge Technologies Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings are volatile or negative, the P/S ratio is often a useful way to think about valuation, because it compares what you pay for each dollar of revenue without relying on near term profits.

Higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/S ratio, while slower expected growth or higher risk usually lines up with a lower, more conservative multiple. So context really matters when you compare any single number.

SolarEdge Technologies currently trades on a P/S ratio of 1.93x. That sits well below the broader Semiconductor industry average P/S of 6.17x and also below the peer group average of 15.00x. Comparing the current 1.93x P/S with the Fair Ratio of 2.44x suggests the shares are trading below that modelled level.

Approach 1: MetLife Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a company is expected to earn over and above the return that equity investors typically require. In simple terms, it starts from book value, estimates future earnings power on that equity base, subtracts the required return, and then capitalizes those excess profits into an intrinsic value per share.

For MetLife, the model uses a Book Value of $43.33 per share and a Stable EPS of $8.59 per share, based on weighted future Return on Equity estimates from 8 analysts. The Cost of Equity is set at $4.35 per share, which implies an Excess Return of $4.24 per share. The Average Return on Equity feeding into these estimates is 15.83%, with a Stable Book Value of $54.27 per share, sourced from weighted future Book Value estimates from 6 analysts.

Feeding these inputs into the Excess Returns framework gives an estimated intrinsic value of about $146.09 per share, compared with a current share price around $77.52. That gap implies the stock screens as roughly 46.9% undervalued using this method.
